evidence: "I enjoy the homage to Hitchcock that it strived for."
"This is one of the best Prime movies on here; it's so good I would have happily paid to watch it."
"It had a lot of fun twists that just kept coming."
"I never knew what was going to happen next -- which is rare, because I watch a lot of movies and can usually figure out what's going to happen in the first 15 minutes."
"This movie is SLOW -- but Dennis Quaid is very creepy."
"I fell asleep both times I tried watching this."
"The movie shows the progression of the illness as Steve races to make journal entries to communicate with his son..."
"The animation is GORGEOUS too, especially the scenes shot on Easter Island..."
"I personally enjoy a movie more when there are unknowns so they can just be characters without your remembering them from other things."

"I found myself wanting to scream for help for her and I felt frustrated that not only was this just a movie but it was also set so far in the past that there's nothing I could have ever done for this girl."
"The kind of horror this is is not the Scream or Friday the 13th variety...it is somber, sober, and plays out slowly..."
"I was so invested emotionally in a character like that."
"This movie will leave you numb, horrified, and maybe a little depressed."

summary: The user has a strong preference for complex narratives, particularly in the mystery and thriller genres, and values high production quality. They enjoy emotional and thought-provoking content, often resonating deeply with the characters and stories presented. However, they have mixed feelings about pacing, indicating a tolerance for both slow and fast narratives depending on the context.
